mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, invalidating the risk-off premise.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative when both series show positive month-over-month changes, indicating the interaction signal is spurious.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals supply-side contraction and weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive despite consecutive monthly declines in industrial production, refuting the supply-demand contraction link.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings and credit availability fuel consumer spending, supporting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 2-year rolling window despite rising savings, indicating the signal is dominated by other liquidity factors.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces prolonged monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when inflation accelerates above the threshold, proving discount rate effects are offset by earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth combined with stable unemployment indicates a resilient labor market, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative when payroll growth exceeds 150k and unemployment remains below 5.0%, invalidating the labor market confidence channel.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing permits signals a sharp downturn in construction activity and consumer wealth, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive when housing permits drop below 1000 units, demonstrating the housing signal lacks predictive power for short-term equity moves.
